title: T-113 — packages/gdpr erasure registry + dispatcher (P0 blocker for §13 GDPR success criterion)
prompt:
# T-113 — packages/gdpr erasure registry + dispatcher

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §13 Compliance ("GDPR right-to-erasure function działa"), §11 Audit log (retention_class), §10 (R13 event-sourced)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `packages/gdpr` (workspace name `@monopilot/gdpr`) exporting a **per-domain erasure registry** + a central `runErasure(orgId, subjectId, opts)` dispatcher that calls every registered domain handler in a single tx-scoped run. PRD §13 lists "GDPR right-to-erasure function działa" as a hard MVP success criterion; today the only implementation is NPD T-089 scoped to NPD tables only. Both auditors (A Top-10 Gap #3, B Top-10 Gap #6) flag this as a Foundation gap.

This task delivers the **framework** (registry + dispatcher + contract + unit tests). The cron caller is T-114 (apps/worker). Wiring NPD's existing handler into the registry is T-115. Other module-domain registrations are downstream (Warehouse, Scanner, Quality, Production, Settings).

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/gdpr/` as workspace package (`@monopilot/gdpr`, private:true, type:module). Deps: `pg` (peer), `@monopilot/db` (peer for schema types), `zod`. Dev: vitest.
2. `packages/gdpr/src/registry.ts` exports `registerErasureHandler(domain: string, fn: ErasureHandler): void` and `getRegisteredHandlers(): ReadonlyMap<string, ErasureHandler>`. Domains are dot-strings (e.g. `npd`, `warehouse`, `quality.holds`). Registry is a module-level singleton; re-registering the same domain throws unless `{force:true}` is passed.
3. `ErasureHandler` signature: `(ctx: ErasureContext) => Promise<ErasureResult>` where `ErasureContext = { orgId: string; subjectId: string; reason: 'gdpr-rtbf'; tx: pg.PoolClient; dryRun: boolean }` and `ErasureResult = { domain: string; rowsAffected: number; tablesTouched: string[]; warnings: string[] }`.
4. `packages/gdpr/src/dispatcher.ts` exports `runErasure(pool: pg.Pool, orgId: string, subjectId: string, opts?: { dryRun?: boolean; domains?: string[] }): Promise<ErasureRunReport>` which (a) BEGINs a transaction on a single client, (b) sets `app.set_org_context(...)` for the orgId, (c) calls each handler sequentially in registration order (or filtered to `opts.domains`), collecting results, (d) writes a single `gdpr_erasure_runs` audit row (action=`gdpr.erasure.completed`, retention_class=`security`) — the dispatcher writes through the existing `audit_events` API, (e) commits if no handler threw, otherwise rolls back and writes `gdpr.erasure.failed` + rethrows.
5. `packages/gdpr/src/__tests__/dispatcher.test.ts` (testcontainers Postgres): register two fake handlers (`testA`, `testB`); call `runErasure(pool, orgId, subjectId)`; assert both invoked exactly once with the same `tx` client; assert `tablesTouched` aggregated correctly; assert one audit event row written with `actor_type='system'` and `action='gdpr.erasure.completed'`. A second test registers a throwing handler and asserts ROLLBACK semantics + failure audit row.
6. Create `_foundation/contracts/gdpr.md` (new contract file) documenting: (a) per-domain handler protocol, (b) order = registration order, (c) all handlers share one tx → all-or-nothing per subject, (d) PII anonymisation policy (`actor_user_id` rows in audit retain via tombstone `gdpr_anonymised_user_id`), (e) the contract is normative for every module that holds user-FK PII (Warehouse `signed_by`, Scanner `operator_id`, Quality e-sign signers, Production WO actor cols), (f) sibling implementations: NPD T-089 + future per-module wire-ups.

## Files in scope
- packages/gdpr/package.json [create]
- packages/gdpr/tsconfig.json [create]
- packages/gdpr/eslint.config.mjs [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/index.ts [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/registry.ts [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/dispatcher.ts [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/types.ts [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/__tests__/registry.test.ts [create]
- packages/gdpr/src/__tests__/dispatcher.test.ts [create]
- _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two fake handlers testA and testB registered, when runErasure(pool, orgId, 'subject-x') awaited, then both invoked exactly once in registration order sharing same tx PoolClient and report.results length is 2.
2. Given a registered handler throws, when runErasure runs, then transaction ROLLBACK, single gdpr.erasure.failed audit row written via separate connection, original error rethrown.
3. Given opts.dryRun=true, when runErasure runs, then handlers receive ctx.dryRun=true, dispatcher COMMITs only the gdpr.erasure.dry_run audit row, no business mutations persist.
4. Given same domain registered twice without {force:true}, when second registerErasureHandler called, then throws DuplicateDomainError with domain name; AND Given _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md is read, when content parsed, then contains handler protocol + tx-scoped contract + audit-event names + sibling implementations + normative scope.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `dispatcher.test.ts` + `registry.test.ts` asserting ACs 1-4 before any production code. RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr test`.
- GREEN: implement registry + dispatcher; testcontainers Postgres with `audit_events` migration pre-applied.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr typecheck && pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not call `app.set_org_context` from outside the dispatcher — handlers MUST receive the already-context-set `tx` client.
- Do not run handlers in parallel — sequential, ordered, single tx (subject-erasure is atomic by GDPR design).
- Do not delete `audit_events` rows in any handler — anonymise via tombstone columns, retain rows per retention_class (security=7y).
- Do not include UI in this task — purely server-side; the operator UI lives in 02-Settings P3.
- Do not call this from a Server Action without the apps/worker cron — the request-path caller is forbidden; T-114 wires the cron.
- Do not hardcode domain list — registration is dynamic so module tasks can add handlers without modifying packages/gdpr.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create @monopilot/gdpr package — per-domain ErasureHandler registry + tx-scoped runErasure(orgId, subjectId) dispatcher + _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md normative contract. Foundation-level fulfilment of §13 GDPR success criterion. Cron wiring is T-114; NPD wire-up is T-115.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Module-level singleton registry exposes registerErasureHandler(domain, fn). Dispatcher opens single tx, sets app.set_org_context, runs handlers sequentially in registration order sharing the same tx client; on success writes gdpr.erasure.completed audit row (retention_class=security); on failure ROLLBACK + gdpr.erasure.failed audit row (separate connection). dryRun mode runs handlers with ctx.dryRun=true and commits only the dry_run audit row. Contract file _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md is normative for every module holding user-FK PII.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
acceptance_criteria: Given two fake handlers testA and testB registered, when runErasure(pool, orgId, 'subject-x') awaited, then both invoked exactly once in registration order sharing same tx PoolClient and report.results length is 2.
Given a registered handler throws, when runErasure runs, then transaction ROLLBACK, single gdpr.erasure.failed audit row written via separate connection, original error rethrown.
Given opts.dryRun=true, when runErasure runs, then handlers receive ctx.dryRun=true, dispatcher COMMITs only the gdpr.erasure.dry_run audit row, no business mutations persist.

Given same domain registered twice without {force:true}, when second registerErasureHandler called, then throws DuplicateDomainError with domain name; AND Given _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md is read, when content parsed, then contains handler protocol + tx-scoped contract + audit-event names + sibling implementations + normative scope.
test_strategy: RED: write dispatcher.test.ts + registry.test.ts asserting ACs 1-4 before code; testcontainers Postgres with audit_events migration applied; RED command: pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr test.
GREEN: implement registry + dispatcher + contract markdown.
Run: pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr typecheck && pnpm --filter @monopilot/gdpr lint.
risk_red_lines: Do not call app.set_org_context from outside the dispatcher — handlers receive an already-context-set tx client.
Do not run handlers in parallel — sequential ordered single tx for atomicity.
Do not delete audit_events rows in any handler — anonymise via tombstone columns, retain per retention_class.
Do not include UI in this task — server-side framework only.
Do not call this from a Server Action — apps/worker cron only (T-114).
Do not hardcode domain list — dynamic registration so module tasks add handlers without modifying packages/gdpr.
